notes.note: Faberius, Caesar's secretary, whose large debt to Cicero (the nomen Faberianum) was meant to fund the purchase of the gardens. Cicero awaits his arrival to settle the account.

The genre of the protreptic 'letter to Alexander the Great,' a stock rhetorical exercise (e.g. Isocrates and the so-called Aristotle) advising the young conqueror. Cicero invokes it as the impossible model his own letter of advice to Caesar would have had to match.
Alexander the Great, tutored in his youth by Aristotle, who (Cicero says) grew arrogant and cruel once he was hailed as king --- a pointed warning about Caesar.

Quirinus is the deified Romulus. Caesar had been voted a statue carried in the procession of the gods and a place beside Quirinus, so Cicero calls him sardonically the 'messmate' (contubernalis) of Quirinus's procession --- a jab at his divine honours.

Archidemus (Archedemus) of Tarsus, a Stoic logician; the 'problema Archidemou' was a knotty dialectical problem Cicero had set Atticus. He uses it here as shorthand for the perplexity he no longer feels about the Caesar letter.
Nicias of Cos, a learned grammarian and friend in Cicero's circle, summoned away by Dolabella. He is Cicero's source for the gossip about Cornificia in the next section.
Philologi, scholars and literary men; Cicero pretends to question Nicias about them in order to draw out the real gossip about the marriage proposal.

The Thalna here is the would-be suitor of Cornificia, called modest and frugal; he is distinct from the corrupt juror Thalna of 61 BC (Att. 1.16) and is not otherwise identifiable. FLAG: no clean roster or registry match; left untagged.
Cornificia, daughter of Quintus Cornificius, an older much-married woman floated as a bride; Cicero finds the match unappealing. 'Quintus' here is her father Cornificius, not Cicero's brother.

A sum in sesterces (HS 800,000), reckoned too small a fortune; the women of the family disapproved of the match on that ground. The bare numeral in the Latin (DCCC) is a standard abbreviation for the figure in thousands.
